title: IREN Secures $9.7 Billion AI Cloud Contract with Microsoft
text: IREN Limited announced a significant multi-year partnership with Microsoft on November 3, 2025. The agreement involves providing GPU cloud services valued at approximately $9.7 billion over five years, including a 20% prepayment. IREN will supply Microsoft with access to NVIDIA GB300 GPUs, with Dell Technologies providing the hardware for roughly $5.8 billion. The GPUs will be rolled out in phases through 2026 at IREN's Childress, Texas campus, which operates at 750MW capacity. The infrastructure will support 200MW of critical IT load across four horizon phases. IREN plans to finance the capital expenditures through existing cash reserves, customer prepayments, operating cash flows, and additional financing arrangements. Daniel Roberts, IREN's Co-Founder & Co-CEO, emphasized that the partnership demonstrates their vertically integrated AI Cloud platform while opening doors to hyperscaler customers. He highlighted the milestone's importance for expanding GPU deployments across their 3GW secured power portfolio in North America. Jonathan Tinter from Microsoft noted that IREN's integrated approach combining data centers with GPU infrastructure and secured power capacity made them an ideal strategic partner.
